UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile on the SwapIt platform, storing their public information and references to their activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

location.description: The user's general location (e.g., city, region) for local swap matching.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

joinDate.description: The date and time when the user registered on the platform.

listedItemIds.description: References to items currently listed by this user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Item)

participatedSwapIds.description: References to successfully completed swaps this user has participated in. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Swap)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents an item listed by a user for swapping.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns and listed this item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Item)

title.description: The short, descriptive title of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item, potentially refined by AI.

condition.description: The current physical condition of the item (e.g., 'New', 'Used - Like New', 'Used - Good', 'Used - Fair').

category.description: The category to which the item belongs (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Books', 'Clothing').

imageUrls.description: URLs to images showcasing the item.

listedDate.description: The date and time when the item was first listed on the platform.

status.description: The current availability status of the item (e.g., 'available', 'pending swap', 'swapped', 'archived').

location.description: The geographical location where the item is available for swap. Can be specific or general.

viewCount.description: The number of times this item listing has been viewed.

preferredSwapCategories.description: Optional: Categories of items the owner is primarily interested in swapping for this item.

SwapProposal.title: SwapProposal
SwapProposal.description: Represents a proposal made by one user to swap one or more of their items for another user's item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SwapProposal entity.

proposerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this swap proposal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SwapProposal)

receiverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns the target item and is receiving this proposal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SwapProposal)

targetItemId.description: Reference to the Item that the proposer wishes to acquire. (Relationship: Item 1:N SwapProposal)

offeredItemIds.description: References to one or more items the proposer is offering in exchange for the target item. (Relationship: Item N:N SwapProposal - where N items are offered for 1 target item)

proposalDate.description: The date and time when the swap proposal was created.

status.description: The current status of the proposal (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'declined', 'withdrawn', 'canceled').

message.description: An optional message from the proposer to the receiver regarding the swap.

acceptanceDate.description: The date and time when the proposal was accepted or declined. Null if pending.

Swap.title: Swap
Swap.description: Represents a successfully completed swap transaction between two users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Swap entity.

proposalId.description: Reference to the SwapProposal that was accepted and led to this completed swap. (Relationship: SwapProposal 1:1 Swap)

initiatorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who originally proposed the swap.

recipientUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who received the original proposal.

initiatorOfferedItemIds.description: References to the items provided by the initiator in this completed swap.

recipientOfferedItemIds.description: References to the items provided by the recipient in this completed swap (typically the target item(s) from the proposal).

swapDate.description: The date and time when the swap was officially marked as completed.

status.description: The final status of the completed swap (e.g., 'fulfilled', 'dispute', 'archived').

notes.description: Any additional notes or details about the completed swap.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the user's authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'id' field matching the document ID for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: The primary storage for items owned by a specific user. This path enforces strong ownership. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' field matching the 'userId' path parameter for authorization independence and simplified rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the item.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the item, matching the 'id' field within the document.

definition.description: A denormalized, global collection of all publicly available items, optimized for browsing, searching, and filtering across all users. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence on write operations (only owner can modify/delete).

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the item, matching the 'id' field within the document.

definition.description: Stores individual swap proposals between users. Includes denormalized 'proposerId' and 'receiverId' fields for authorization independence, allowing both parties to manage the proposal without hierarchical lookups.

params.name: swapProposalId
params.description: The unique identifier of the swap proposal, matching the 'id' field within the document.

definition.description: Records successfully completed swap transactions. Includes denormalized 'initiatorUserId' and 'recipientUserId' fields for authorization independence, granting both participants access to the swap record without hierarchical lookups.

params.name: swapId
params.description: The unique identifier of the completed swap, matching the 'id' field within the document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for justSwap prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation. 

**Authorization Independence:** Every document critical for authorization (Item, SwapProposal, Swap) explicitly contains all necessary user IDs (`ownerId`, `proposerId`, `receiverId`, `initiatorUserId`, `recipientUserId`) directly within the document itself. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which are prone to breaking atomic operations (transactions, batches) and are difficult to debug. For instance, an `Item` document will always have its `ownerId` field, allowing rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.ownerId` for write operations, regardless of its path. Similarly, `SwapProposal` and `Swap` documents contain both participating user IDs, enabling simple `request.auth.uid == resource.data.proposerId || request.auth.uid == resource.data.receiverId` type checks.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **`/users/{userId}`:** This collection stores `UserProfile` documents. It follows a private data model, where `userId` maps directly to `request.auth.uid`. This path's security rules will be simple: users can only read/write their own profile data, ensuring strong path-based ownership. Other users might have read access to certain public fields of a profile.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/items/{itemId}`:** This subcollection holds the master records of items owned by a specific user. This structure strongly enforces ownership, making it easy to define rules where `request.auth.uid` must match `userId` in the path and `resource.data.ownerId`. While providing strong ownership, querying all items across all users from this structure would require collection group queries.
3.  **`/items/{itemId}`:** To support the "Browse and Search Items" core feature, a top-level `/items` collection is introduced. This collection acts as a denormalized, public view of all available items. Its documents are duplicates or subsets of the `Item` data from `/users/{userId}/items/{itemId}`. The primary purpose of this collection is to enable efficient, global `list` operations (QAPs) with filters (e.g., by category, location). Authorization for write operations (create, update, delete) on these documents will still rely on the denormalized `ownerId` field, ensuring only the item's owner can modify it, while read access can be granted to all authenticated users (or even unauthenticated if items are public).
4.  **`/swapProposals/{swapProposalId}`:** Swap proposals are stored in a top-level collection because they involve two users (`proposerId`, `receiverId`) and don't cleanly fit under a single user's path for all queries. Each document contains both `proposerId` and `receiverId`, allowing robust security rules that grant access to either participant. This design supports QAPs for users to efficiently query their incoming and outgoing proposals by filtering on these fields.
5.  **`/swaps/{swapId}`:** Similar to `SwapProposal`, completed `Swap` transactions are stored in a top-level collection. Each document includes `initiatorUserId` and `recipientUserId` for clear authorization. This supports QAPs for users to query their past transactions. 

By employing this strategy, Firestore rules become much simpler and more robust, primarily relying on direct field checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`) instead of complex and less performant `get()` operations or brittle hierarchical dependencies. The separation of `Item` data into an owner-specific subcollection and a global public collection addresses the need for both strong ownership and global discoverability/queryability (QAPs) efficiently.
